title: How to Create a Standard Work Order
author: Neela Thiagarajan

overview.content:
A Standard Work Order (SWO) in the EAM system serves as the work instruction template for WGC (corrective maintenance) campaigns. The SWO number must match the Campaign number exactly — this is how the system links work instructions, billing codes, and checklist items to generated work orders.

An SWO is created before a WGC Campaign. It references a Task Plan via the Activities tab, which defines what technicians do, which billing codes apply, and which documents appear on handheld devices.

SWOs are reusable across multiple campaigns, but in practice each campaign typically references a unique SWO. The SWO number drives downstream reporting — ODS aggregates work order data by SWO number, so naming consistency matters.

Used exclusively with WGC (corrective, one-time) campaigns. PMC (preventive, recurring) campaigns use PM Schedules instead.
overview.process_stage: Task Plan Creation → Standard Work Order Creation → WGC Campaign Creation
overview.business_outcome: Corrective maintenance work orders generated with correct work instructions, billing codes, and documents for targeted equipment
overview.data_triggers: SWO number must equal Campaign number · Task Plan must be linked on Activities tab before campaign generates work orders · SWO drives ODS reporting aggregation

prerequisites.content:
Before creating a Standard Work Order you must have:

1. Task Plan already created and saved — have the Task Plan number ready
   The Task Plan defines the checklist items, billing codes, and documents
   that will appear on all generated work orders

2. SWO number decided — this MUST equal the Campaign number exactly
   Plan the naming convention before creating (e.g. CBM-602, TEST-0001)

3. Access to the EAM system with Standard Work Order creation permissions

4. Campaign Car List or filter criteria defined — know your target equipment
   population before creating the SWO and campaign

5. Sub Work Type confirmed if required — obtain permissible values from
   Process SME before creating
prerequisites.business_outcome: Standard Work Order created correctly on first attempt — no rework from naming mismatch or missing Task Plan
prerequisites.data_triggers: SWO number must equal Campaign number — plan before creating · Task Plan must exist before SWO can link to it

steps.content:
Steps to Create a Standard Work Order:

STEP 1 — Navigate and Create New Record:
1. Navigate: Work > Work Order Planning > Standard Work Orders
2. Click the New Record icon
3. Enter the SWO number — this MUST match the Campaign number exactly
4. Enter a Description — clear and searchable

STEP 2 — Link the Task Plan (Activities tab):
1. Navigate to the Activities tab
2. Click Add Activity
3. Set Trade = STAR
4. Set Quantity = 1
5. In the Task Plan field — search and select the Task Plan for this work
6. Click Save
Note: The Task Plan must already exist before this step

STEP 3 — Verify Task Plan content carried over:
1. Confirm checklist items are visible on the Activities tab
2. Confirm billing relief codes are present
3. Confirm primary document reference is attached

STEP 4 — Save:
1. Click Save Record
2. Note the SWO number — this is the number you will use for the Campaign

VERIFY:
- SWO visible in list view
- Task Plan linked on Activities tab
- SWO number matches your planned Campaign number
steps.process_stage: Standard Work Order Creation → Link Task Plan → WGC Campaign Creation
steps.business_outcome: Standard Work Order created with Task Plan linked — ready for WGC Campaign to reference
steps.data_triggers: SWO number must equal Campaign number · Task Plan linked via Activities tab (trade STAR, qty 1) · SWO drives work order content at generation time

rules.content:
Critical Rules — Standard Work Order:

SWO NUMBER MUST EQUAL CAMPAIGN NUMBER EXACTLY
The SWO number and Campaign number must match exactly — no spaces, exact format.
Wrong number = work orders generated without correct content.

TASK PLAN MUST BE LINKED BEFORE CAMPAIGN RUNS
Task Plan must be linked on Activities tab (trade STAR, qty 1) before the WGC
Campaign generates work orders. If linked after generation, existing work orders
will not have checklist items or billing codes.

ONE TASK PLAN PER SWO
Each SWO should reference one Task Plan. Multiple Task Plans causes unpredictable
work order content.

SWO IS REUSABLE — PLAN NAMING CAREFULLY
SWO can be referenced by multiple campaigns using suffix naming (CBM-602-01,
CBM-602-02) all referencing SWO CBM-602. ODS aggregates by SWO number.

SAVE IS REQUIRED AFTER EVERY STATUS CHANGE
Status change alone does not trigger any system action. Save must be clicked after
every status change.
rules.process_stage: Standard Work Order Creation → WGC Campaign Creation
rules.business_outcome: Work order generation errors prevented — correct content on all generated work orders
rules.data_triggers: SWO number drives campaign linking · Task Plan on Activities tab at time of WO generation · Save required after every status change

troubleshoot.content:
Standard Work Order Troubleshooting:

Problem: Work orders generated without checklist items or billing codes
Cause: Task Plan was not linked on the SWO Activities tab before the campaign ran, or wrong Task Plan was linked.
Fix: Open the SWO → Activities tab. Verify the correct Task Plan is linked (trade STAR, qty 1). Note: already-generated work orders will not update — only new work orders from this point forward will pick up the Task Plan content.

Problem: Work orders generated with wrong content
Cause: SWO number did not match Campaign number — system linked the wrong SWO.
Fix: Verify SWO number exactly matches Campaign number. No spaces, exact format. If mismatch occurred, the campaign may need to be recreated with the correct SWO reference.

Problem: SWO not found when creating Campaign
Cause: SWO number entered in Campaign does not match any existing SWO, or SWO is in wrong status.
Fix: Search for SWO in Work Order Planning. Confirm the exact number including any prefix or suffix. Confirm SWO is in Active or correct status.

Problem: Task Plan lookup returns no results on Activities tab
Cause: Task Plan Work Order Type is not set to Campaign.
Fix: Open the Task Plan and verify Work Order Type = Campaign. See MAINT-TP-001 for Task Plan rules.

Problem: Downstream reporting not showing work orders under correct SWO
Cause: Campaign was created with a different number than the SWO, or suffix naming was inconsistent.
Fix: Check ODS reporting aggregation — it groups by SWO number. Ensure all related campaigns use the same SWO base number with consistent suffix naming (CBM-602-01, CBM-602-02).
troubleshoot.process_stage: Standard Work Order Creation → WGC Campaign Creation
troubleshoot.business_outcome: Work order generation errors prevented — correct content on all generated work orders
troubleshoot.data_triggers: Task Plan linked at time of WO generation · SWO number must match Campaign number · ODS aggregates by SWO number

glossary.content:
Standard Work Order Glossary:

Standard Work Order (SWO): The work instruction template for a WGC corrective maintenance campaign. The SWO number must exactly match the Campaign number. References a Task Plan via the Activities tab.

Activities Tab: The tab on a Standard Work Order where the Task Plan is linked. Trade must be set to STAR, quantity 1. This is the connection point between the SWO and the work instructions that appear on generated work orders.

Trade STAR: The trade designation used when linking a Task Plan to a Standard Work Order on the Activities tab. Must be set to STAR for the Task Plan content to carry through to work orders.

WGC Campaign: Corrective maintenance campaign that references an SWO to generate one-time work orders for a targeted equipment set. The Campaign number must equal the SWO number.

ODS (Reporting System): Downstream reporting system that aggregates work order data by SWO number. Naming consistency across related campaigns is critical for accurate reporting.

Suffix Naming Convention: When splitting a large equipment population across multiple campaigns, all campaigns reference the same SWO using incremental suffixes (CBM-602-01, CBM-602-02). ODS aggregates all of them under the base SWO number.

Campaign Number: Must exactly match the SWO number. This is how the system identifies which work instructions, billing codes, and checklist items to apply to generated work orders.
glossary.process_stage: Standard Work Order Creation → WGC Campaign Creation
glossary.business_outcome: Work order generation errors prevented — correct content on all generated work orders
glossary.data_triggers: Term definitions — reference only, no system actions triggered

default_process_stage: Standard Work Order Creation → WGC Campaign Creation
default_outcome: Work order generation errors prevented — correct content on all generated work orders
